stories.title: FY2026-27 Budget Public Input Period Is Open
stories.excerpt: Durham County is accepting public comment on the proposed FY2026-27 budget. Submit written comments online at dconc.gov or attend the public hearing on May 26 at 7 PM at Commissioners Chambers, 200 E. Main St. County Manager Claudia Hager presents the recommended budget on May 11.

stories.title: Durham Public Schools Submits FY2026-27 Budget Request to County
stories.excerpt: The DPS Board of Education submitted its FY2026-27 budget request to the county, seeking increased funding for staffing, facilities, and mental health services. See the board's agenda materials for the full request.

stories.title: FY2026-27 Budget Schedule: Key Dates for Public Participation
stories.excerpt: County Manager Claudia Hager presents the recommended FY2026-27 budget on May 11 at 7 PM. The public hearing is May 26 at 7 PM at Commissioners Chambers, 200 E. Main St. The Board of Commissioners votes on adoption June 8. See dconc.gov for the full schedule.

stories.title: Planning Commission Reviews Southside Rezoning at April 23 Meeting
stories.excerpt: The City-County Planning Commission will take up a rezoning application in the Southside neighborhood on April 23. See the Planning Commission agenda for full application details.

stories.title: BOCC Takes Up Affordable Housing Bond Question
stories.excerpt: The Board of Commissioners is considering an affordable housing bond measure for the November ballot. The bond would fund new housing units for residents earning below 60% of area median income. No final vote has been taken.

stories.title: GoTriangle Pursues Federal Grant for Durham–Chapel Hill BRT
stories.excerpt: GoTriangle is seeking federal Small Starts funding for the Durham–Chapel Hill Bus Rapid Transit project. The BRT line would run dedicated bus lanes on NC-54 between the two cities. A funding decision from the FTA is expected later this year.

stories.title: Durham County Expands Crisis Intervention Program Countywide
stories.excerpt: Durham County is rolling out its co-responder crisis program to all patrol districts starting May 1. Deputies are paired with licensed mental health clinicians on calls involving mental health emergencies. The program was first used in East Durham.

stories.title: Ellerbe Creek Greenway Extension Breaks Ground
stories.excerpt: Durham Parks & Recreation broke ground on an Ellerbe Creek Greenway extension on April 12. See the Parks & Recreation website for the latest project details.

stories.title: Durham County Seeks Applicants for Housing Advisory Board
stories.excerpt: Durham County is looking for residents to serve on its Housing Advisory Board, which advises commissioners on affordable housing policy. Applications are due May 1 at dconc.gov.

stories.title: Free Vaccination Clinic at Durham Human Services on June 9
stories.excerpt: The Durham County Health Department is hosting a free vaccination clinic on June 9 from 10 AM to 2 PM at 414 E. Main St. Flu, COVID-19, and standard adult vaccines will be available. No appointment needed.
